summary: Fatal runtime error blocks the entire Home page. `themeHint` is referenced in Home.jsx (line 631) but never declared as state — Home component throws 'ReferenceError: themeHint is not defined' and renders a blank white page. None of the Fall/theme flows are reachable in the browser. All subsequent testids (theme-menu-button, theme-menu, spin-roulette-button, zip-input, fall-scene, etc.) are absent from the DOM.

ui_bugs.issue: ReferenceError: themeHint is not defined at Home.jsx:631 — variable is used inside JSX for the one-time theme hint but there is no useState/useRef/const declaration for it (and no setter). Home ErrorBoundary catches it and renders nothing, so the whole page is blank on every load.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot verify Theme menu, Fall scene, Fall CTA color, Light/Dark regression, shuffle leaf cards, or hint bubble because the page never mounts.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Home.jsx add the missing themeHint state (and its dismissal wiring): e.g. `const [themeHint, setThemeHint] = useState(() => !localStorage.getItem('ff_theme_hint_seen'));` plus a useEffect that clears it after N seconds and a call to setThemeHint(false) + localStorage.setItem('ff_theme_hint_seen','1') when the theme menu is opened or an option is chosen.

After the fix, verify (a) hint appears on fresh load, disappears after open/select and does NOT reappear on reload, (b) Theme menu renders 3 options with checkmark on active, (c) Fall scene DOM images present, (d) Fall spin CTA bg ≈ rgb(192,69,27), Light ≈ rgb(168,201,158), Dark ≈ rgb(224,30,38), (e) shuffle card-back contains /leaf-* img in Fall.
Add an ErrorBoundary that surfaces the error message to avoid a fully white page on future regressions.
critical_code_review_comments: Undeclared identifier `themeHint` used in JSX on line 631 of Home.jsx — likely a missed rebase/paste. No corresponding useState or import.
Home.jsx is very large; consider extracting the theme-menu + hint into a small ThemeSwitcher component so this class of bug is caught by lint/isolated tests.
Consider enabling `no-undef` ESLint rule for JSX identifiers so this fails CI instead of at runtime.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Home.jsx line 631 references undefined `themeHint`. Once main agent declares the state, re-run the full 8-check theme suite. Places API budget already unused (0 calls made) because page never mounted.
rca of the issue: Root cause: Missing state declaration for `themeHint` in Home.jsx. Reproduction: open REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — console shows `PAGE ERROR: themeHint is not defined` and the React error boundary reports 'An error occurred in the <Home> component'. Page renders blank. Mitigation: add `const [themeHint, setThemeHint] = useState(...)` and its dismissal effects/handlers as described in action items.
